name: Minhoto Portuguese
notes: Northern interior dialect (Minho province: Braga, Guimarães, Vizela). Phonological features diverge substantially from standard EP: (1) extreme unstressed vowel reduction — /ɐ/, /ə/, /u/ delete or devoice word-finally and inter-consonantally; (2) voiceless uvular fricative [χ] in coda position where standard has [ʁ]; (3) retroflex flap [ɽ] as an allophone of /ɾ/ in certain environments; (4) partial retroflex sibilants [ʂ, ʐ] — less systematic than Transmontano; (5) strong nasal vowels with preserved nasality contrast; (6) open vowel qualities [ɛ, ɔ] in stressed position broader than Lisbon standard; (7) /t/ aspirated before high front vowels [tʰi]; (8) /d/ deleted intervocalically in informal speech. Prosodically: stress-timed rhythm with heavy syllable compression. Classified as a northern EP dialect under Cintra's dialect geography; shares features with Galician-Portuguese contact zone.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Minhoto diverges mainly in vowel reduction and uvular coda realization

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
